narrative:
Moisés Caicedo is a key defensive midfielder for Ecuador, holding an impressive overall rating of 7.25/10. This rating places him in the top 2% of defensive midfields at this tournament. His current performance assessment is a blend of 61% club form and 39% from this World Cup.

Caicedo has played all 360 World Cup minutes, demonstrating his importance to the squad. His attacking output is notable for a defensive midfielder, with 0.467 expected assists and 2.25 key passes per 90 minutes at this World Cup. Defensively, he contributes 2.25 tackles and 1.0 interception per 90 minutes. However, his expected goals per 90 minutes at this World Cup stand at a modest 0.021, reflecting a limited direct goal threat.

Caicedo clearly operates as a vital engine for Ecuador, anchoring the midfield defensively while also driving forward play. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his consistent defensive contributions in tackles and interceptions. Furthermore, watch for his ability to unlock defenses with key passes and convert his high expected assists into tangible goal contributions in upcoming matches.
